title: Just Chat Range
description: This mod lets you add proximity chat for text! (works on advancements, kills/deaths, text messages)
body:
# Chat Range

A server-side Fabric mod for Minecraft 1.21.11 that limits chat visibility to a configurable block radius. Players only see messages from others who are nearby — including chat, death messages, notifications, and advancement announcements.

## Requirements

- Minecraft 1.21.11
- Fabric Loader 0.18.0+
- Fabric API 0.141.3+1.21.11

## Installation

1. Install [Fabric Loader](https://fabricmc.net/use/installer/) for Minecraft 1.21.11.
2. Download [Fabric API](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api) for 1.21.11 and place it in your `mods/` folder.
3. Place `chatrange-1.0.0.jar` in your server's `mods/` folder.
4. Start the server.

The mod is **server-side only** — clients do not need to install anything.

## Commands

All commands require operator permissions (gamemaster level).

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `/chatrange set <blocks>` | Enable range-limited chat with the given radius |
| `/chatrange on` | Re-enable with the last configured radius |
| `/chatrange off` | Disable — all messages are broadcast globally |
| `/chatrange status` | Show whether the mod is enabled and the current radius |

The radius is in blocks. Example: `/chatrange set 100` limits chat to a 100-block radius.

## What is filtered

When enabled, the following messages are limited to players within the configured radius of the source:

- **Player chat** — messages typed in chat
- **Command messages** — `/say`, `/me`, etc.
- **Death messages** — only nearby players see when someone dies
- **Advancement announcements** — only nearby players see advancement completions

Messages from the server console are always broadcast globally.

## Configuration

Settings are saved automatically to `config/chatrange.json` on your server:

```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "range": 64.0
}
```

You can edit this file directly while the server is stopped, or use the in-game commands while it's running.

The default radius is **64 blocks**. The mod starts **disabled** on first install — use `/chatrange set <blocks>` or `/chatrange on` to activate it.
